statusDefinitions.implemented: The exact bounded scope has executable repository evidence. This does not confer support on adjacent Haxe semantics or make a release promise.
statusDefinitions.scaffold-only: A typed contract, seed, fixture, or plan exists, but it is not evidence of an available user-program capability.
statusDefinitions.experimental: The surface is explicit and opt-in, remains unstable, and has not passed a supported-release capability gate.
statusDefinitions.unsupported: The surface is absent, deliberately fails closed, or lacks the evidence needed for a product claim.

compatibility.profiles: This manifest describes evidence only and does not change portable or metal semantics.
compatibility.runtimePolicy: This manifest selects no hxrt feature and does not change auto, minimal, or none resolution.
compatibility.environments: Native fixture evidence is not a supported environment or platform tuple; no support matrix is claimed.
compatibility.generatedC: Only the separately admitted primitive and closed-generic static-function, local fixed-array/span storage with bounded nonescaping internal span parameters, closed anonymous-record, bounded direct-value enum, and compiler-known literal-output slices have generated-C evidence; the Caxecraft domain composes only that bounded runtime-free subset.
compatibility.publicAbi: The target-facing vocabulary is provisional and no generated public C ABI is stable or supported.

claimGates.reason: The current ledger is a planning seed and general standard-library calls remain unsupported.

Only bounded primitive and closed-generic static-function, local fixed-array/span storage with nonescaping internal span parameters, closed anonymous-record, direct-value enum, and compiler-known literal-output subsets reach generated C; hello exercises literal output and the Caxecraft domain composes the runtime-free subset without broadening it.
No versioned benchmark methodology, dashboard, or regression budget has passed.
Security, compatibility, stdlib, platform, release, and broad semantic gates remain open.
No Haxe export analyzer, generated public header, ABI manifest, or compatibility policy is complete.
Current native CI is component evidence, not the complete ADR 0007 environment/runtime/architecture/compiler tuple matrix.
No publishable, signed, reproducible, evidence-backed release exists.

doctor.remediation: Use the documented repository validation commands today. E8.T06 must make future human and JSON doctor output consume these inventory IDs rather than maintaining a second status list.

capabilities.summary: Repository governance tooling validates and previews the checked-in Beads plan deterministically; this is not compiler or runtime functionality.
capabilities.scope: The plan contains stable epic/task keys, blocking edges, requirement coverage, and an idempotent materializer.
limitations: This is workflow infrastructure, not compiler or runtime behavior.

capabilities.summary: No Clang-backed binding generator or user bindgen command exists.
capabilities.scope: The PRD and Beads graph reserve deterministic parsing, mapping, locking, ABI probing, and wrapper work.
limitations: Independent native C fixtures are not generated bindings.

capabilities.summary: A schema-checked M0 product, infrastructure, and limitation inventory drives the README status table.
capabilities.scope: The manifest fixes status meanings, evidence paths, ownership, hard claim gates, and future doctor consumption.
limitations: It reports evidence and changes no compiler behavior.

capabilities.summary: One typed neutral executable plan deterministically drives optional CMake and Meson files plus a direct argument-array consumer.
capabilities.scope: Schema 1 records strict C mode, owned sources and headers, include directories, typed definitions, logical libraries, pkg-config packages, frameworks, and source provenance once for every consumer.
limitations: The schema-1 seed emits one non-installing executable target; library artifacts, exports, toolchain and sysroot configuration, cross-compilation, and CLI orchestration remain E7/E8 work.
Building the structural C AST/printer corpus does not prove general Haxe lowering, a public ABI, platform support, or release readiness.

capabilities.summary: Target-owned AST corpora deterministically emit and native-run single-file and header/source C projects.
capabilities.scope: Direct typed Haxe builders cover declarations, precedence, initializers, control flow, explicit extension attributes, and guarded header/source output through Reflaxe ownership. A reusable argument-array harness records compiler identity, normalized commands, hashes, and process results.
limitations: Direct AST fixtures alone do not prove Haxe-language lowering.
Compiler attributes remain explicit GNU/Clang extension evidence; strict ISO C11 is still the normative default.

capabilities.summary: No Haxe export analyzer, public-header generator, library packaging, or ABI diff workflow exists.
capabilities.scope: The Beads E7 plan owns exported declarations, ABI-safe types, wrappers, libraries, manifests, and consumers.
limitations: Independent native C/C++ consumers are boundary seeds, not generated export evidence.

capabilities.summary: The c23 internal dialect spelling is explicit and opt-in but has no support-matrix claim.
capabilities.scope: Configuration recognizes c23 while public headers remain constrained to the C11 contract.
limitations: It has no independent native syntax matrix and cannot change semantics or ABI.

capabilities.summary: Concrete non-generic Haxe classes emit private base-prefix structs with checked nullable-reference operations and safe upcasts.
capabilities.scope: Source-order own fields, embedded base storage, empty-root anchors, inherited reads/writes, identity, explicit null proofs, and null-preserving derived-to-base conversions lower through schema-10 HxcIR and run as runtime-free strict C11.
limitations: Bounded nonescaping stack construction and closed-world virtual dispatch are separate capabilities; general allocation, interface/dynamic dispatch, generic classes, runtime descriptors, reflection/type tests, ownership/GC, and public class ABI remain fail-closed.
The generated program header and every asserted layout are private compiler implementation details, not stable exports.

capabilities.summary: A bounded closed anonymous-record graph emits deduplicated private value structs and runs as runtime-free strict C11.
capabilities.scope: Primitive and nested closed-record fields retain source-order construction, canonical structural identity, explicit copies, projections, field addresses, and native-checked private layout.
limitations: Void fields, record mutation/identity, open or reflective shapes, public ABI structs, and packed metal layouts remain fail-closed; nominal class references use the separate class-layout capability.

capabilities.summary: A target-neutral typed core strictly parses schema-1 hxc.json, resolves six precedence layers, and reports every effective value with provenance.
capabilities.scope: The bounded core owns closed project and overlay keys, independent profile/runtime/environment/C/build/artifact axes, config-root-relative paths, canonical defaults and profile presets, CLI/direct-define override inputs, and deterministic inspection JSON.
limitations: The E8.T02 command router and E8.T04 native build driver do not yet expose hxc build, run, test, clean, inspect, or watch commands.
Configured library artifact kinds and non-hosted/toolchain tuples are typed intent only until their build/export adapters pass their separate capability gates.

Direct Haxe/HXML compilation remains authoritative; compiler-manifest integration of an hxc-resolved project model is deferred to the build-driver boundary rather than making the compiler read ambient project files.

capabilities.summary: Concrete non-generic Haxe classes support bounded nonescaping stack construction with exact constructor order and explicit failure cleanup.
capabilities.scope: Unconditional entry-block local new expressions lower through schema-10 HxcIR to default-initialized automatic storage and ordinary private C constructor functions; super calls, pinned-Haxe field/body order, trivial-chain elision, typed status propagation, and reverse partial-initialization cleanup are runtime-free and native-verified.
limitations: Escaping, aliased, assigned, returned, passed, or conditional object references, constructor cycles, native layouts, generic/interface construction, heap allocation, general exceptions, interface/dynamic dispatch, object runtime metadata, and public class ABI remain fail-closed.

capabilities.summary: Structural declaration planning handles complete types, forward declarations, includes, and private boundaries deterministically.
capabilities.scope: Test-owned adapters emit independently compilable strict-C11 header plans from typed contract snapshots.
limitations: The planner fixture is not a generated public ABI or general Haxe declaration emitter.

capabilities.summary: Thirteen typed diagnostic IDs and deterministic schema-1 records are registry- and drift-checked.
capabilities.scope: Current bootstrap, configuration, lowering, runtime, target-contract, and internal failures use the target-owned catalog.
limitations: The public hxc JSON event stream, command context, and native-tool attachments remain unimplemented.

capabilities.summary: Reached hand-authored extern declarations directly consume an authoritative C header and library through structural generated C.
capabilities.scope: Exact fixed-arity C functions, scalars including target-qualified binary32 float, typedef identity, direct one-level imported struct aliases, fieldless enum/constants, by-value imported structs and fields, typed aggregate constants, direct closed-literal struct construction through c.StructInit, and borrowed literal CString arguments lower through schema-10 HxcIR to runtime-free strict C11 with provenance-preserving include, link, pkg-config, and framework facts. Float32 narrowing/widening is explicit and generated headers assert the selected target format.
limitations: Declarations may be hand-authored or produced by a purpose-built reviewed generator such as the bounded raylib core; generic Clang-backed bindgen remains unsupported.
Callbacks, variadics, pointers and retained lifetimes, opaque ownership, unions, bit fields, packing, C++, public exports, and stable ABI remain fail-closed.
Compiled pointlib probes establish only the declared fixture header and target compiler invocation, not general cross-platform ABI compatibility.

capabilities.summary: Repository-owned hello and Caxecraft-domain examples compile ordinary Haxe through generated C and run with exact Eval differentials.
capabilities.scope: examples/hello proves the exact four-feature literal-output closure. examples/caxecraft proves a finite 16 KiB voxel world, deterministic terrain/edits/DDA/fixed-step AABB collision, 32 property seeds, compiler-server determinism, split/package/unity layout parity, and allocation-free runtime-none C under strict and sanitized native lanes; it is the renderer-independent core rather than the pending playable Raylib shell.
limitations: The playable Raylib shell, packaged hxc/watch workflow, fresh-install walkthrough, general Haxe or String support, supported platform, performance claim, and stable public ABI remain unproven.
Both examples use reusable compiler paths without raw C injection or an example-specific compiler branch; fixture-only native consumers are not generated application source.

capabilities.summary: Eight evidence lanes and centrally owned deterministic snapshots have fail-closed policy checks.
capabilities.scope: Focused M0/E3/E6 suites, including constructor lowering, virtual dispatch, and direct C import, plus the declared hello and Caxecraft-domain product examples map exact runners and evidence without treating examples as implicit test globs.
limitations: A registered lane proves only its declared artifact and assertion scope.

capabilities.summary: General Haxe programs are not supported and stop at the first unsupported typed construct.
capabilities.scope: Only the separately listed primitive and closed-generic static-function, local fixed-array/span, closed anonymous-record, concrete class-reference/layout, bounded nonescaping constructor, closed-world virtual-dispatch, bounded direct-value enum, compiler-known literal-output, and exact direct-C-import subsets are admitted.
limitations: General or escaping class allocation, unadmitted instance signatures, interface/dynamic calls, closures, generic classes/references or descriptor-driven bodies, general String values, general arrays, catch/finally and exception payloads, and dynamic/reflection semantics remain outside the admitted slice.

capabilities.summary: Admitted projects use deterministic content-addressed artifacts and Reflaxe-owned stale-file handling.
capabilities.scope: The writer rejects path escapes, symlink escapes, unowned collisions, and stale files outside the prior manifest; admitted direct imports reach the same owned project/build/runtime-plan boundary.
limitations: Only wholly admitted compiler graphs may reach this output boundary.

capabilities.summary: Reachable closed generic static functions and admitted generic enums share deterministic program-local instances and emit a bounded code-size report.
capabilities.scope: Direct static calls infer non-null Bool/Int/UInt/Float or admitted enum arguments, expand aliases, keep finite same-nominal nesting distinct, deduplicate full length-prefixed semantic keys, close recursion through request-local worklists, and remain runtime-free under 64 function, 64 type, and 524,288 estimated-C-byte limits.
limitations: Dynamic or open arguments, classes/references, anonymous records, function types, nullability, native pointers, generic instances or code size beyond the hard budgets, and non-stationary recursive enum growth fail with source-positioned HXC1001.
Generic classes, general containers, descriptor-driven shared bodies, boxing, reflection, closures, dynamic dispatch, exported generics, and cross-program specialization stability remain unsupported.

capabilities.summary: Contribution, disclosure, license, vendoring, provenance, and future release responsibilities are drift-checked.
capabilities.scope: The repository has a private reporting path, GPL-3.0-only source policy, and fail-closed third-party inventory checks.
limitations: Governance policy is not a completed security review or release implementation.

capabilities.summary: No Run.hx, hxc command router, project schema, template, or packaged executable exists.
capabilities.scope: Direct Haxe/HXML remains the only user-program compiler invocation; E8 owns the optional orchestration layer.
limitations: Repository Python validation scripts are developer tooling and are not hxc commands.

capabilities.summary: The hxc doctor human and JSON command is not implemented.
capabilities.scope: This manifest provides the capability IDs and current unsupported contract that E8.T06 must consume.
limitations: No tool, SDK, sysroot, compiler, sanitizer, or writable-output probe is exposed as hxc doctor today.

capabilities.summary: Schema-10 HxcIR structurally records values, tagged cases, concrete and header-owned nominal types, reachable virtual layouts/slots/tables, table binding, exact native dispatch/constants, explicit binary32 rounding/binary64 widening, distinct Haxe and borrowed C string literals, ordering, control flow, failures, cleanup, and runtime intent.
capabilities.scope: The validator and deterministic dumper cover the semantic core consumed by the admitted primitive, Float32, fixed-array/span, aggregate, concrete class-reference, bounded constructor, closed-world virtual-dispatch, bounded enum, literal-output, and direct-C-import lowering slices.
limitations: An IR node does not imply frontend lowering or C emission exists for that semantic feature.

capabilities.summary: Compiler-known String literals support hosted Sys.println and default trace with exact UTF-8/NUL bytes and explicit output failure handling.
capabilities.scope: Portable auto and metal minimal builds lower literal-only output through HxcIR, package only runtime-base/status/string-literal/io, and run as strict C11.
limitations: Only compiler-known valid String literals are admitted; general String values, Dynamic formatting, Sys.print, custom trace parameters, and trace rebinding still fail closed.
The feature is hosted-only and does not establish general standard-library, platform, release, or public runtime ABI support.

capabilities.summary: Independent C-library and C++ extern-C shim inputs validate interop boundary shapes.
capabilities.scope: Both native inputs compile and run independently; pointlib also serves as the authoritative external library for the bounded generated-Haxe direct-import proof.
limitations: The C++ shim remains independent seed evidence; neither fixture proves bindgen, export, or a stable public ABI.

capabilities.summary: Strict GCC/G++ and Clang/Clang++ CI lanes compile and run the declared structural, generated, runtime, import, aggregate/class/constructor/dispatch/enum-layout, generic-specialization, hello, and Caxecraft-domain corpus.
capabilities.scope: The matrix uses strict C11/C++17 flags and exercises the precise fixture set, direct pointlib import plus compiled ABI probe, private aggregate, class, constructor-header, virtual-dispatch-header, and enum C/C++ agreement, checked class-reference, constructor-failure, and indirect-call behavior, closed generic C11 execution, the checked-in generated hello and allocation-free Caxecraft-domain baselines, and seeded primitive/Caxecraft ASan/UBSan corpora registered by the runners.
limitations: Component-native evidence is not a complete supported-platform or release matrix.

capabilities.summary: No compiler-time, C-compile-time, runtime, size, allocation, FFI, or agent benchmark claim is validated.
capabilities.scope: Future versioned methodology and budgets must precede performance or hand-written-quality marketing claims.
limitations: Code-shape tests and deterministic output are not performance benchmarks.

capabilities.summary: No operating-system, architecture, environment, runtime, and compiler tuple is currently a supported release lane.
capabilities.scope: ADR 0007 records the future Tier 1 gate; current local and CI compilers provide component evidence only.
limitations: Cross compilation or one native fixture cannot establish a supported platform tuple.

capabilities.summary: A bounded primitive static-function graph with deterministic initialization, typed direct exact/modulo integer conversions, and literal or bounded zero-initialized local fixed-array/span storage plus nonescaping internal span parameters emits and runs runtime-free strict C11.
capabilities.scope: The slice covers typed constants, locals, globals, dependency-ordered class/static initialization, direct calls, range-preserving `c.IntConvert.exact` and unsigned-target `c.IntConvert.modulo` operations, sequenced control flow, UB-safe arithmetic, fixed arrays with compile-time size/bounds policy, exact-width spans, and mutable or const spans forwarded through reached direct static calls as an internal pointer-plus-length pair; its primitive operations have seeded Eval/ASan/UBSan evidence and the Caxecraft domain composes them into a 16 KiB finite voxel workload.
limitations: Invalid, helper-requiring signed-target, checked, failing, and target-ABI integer conversions remain outside the executable slice.

Span parameters are limited to reached non-generic direct static implementation calls; storage, returns, recursive or indirect calls, instance or virtual dispatch, callbacks, native calls, and public exports remain fail-closed.
The scope is not general Haxe, standard-library parity, object semantics, or evidence for the pending playable Raylib shell.

capabilities.summary: A typed schema-3 primitive representation, nullability, conversion, and operation contract has independent and generated C evidence.
capabilities.scope: Void, Bool, Int, UInt, binary64 Float, exact binary32 c.Float32, exact and ABI integers, and the registered operation slice preserve explicit semantics without hxrt. Float32 conversion is explicit, target-guarded, and boundary-tested through direct imports.
limitations: The contract does not cover strings, objects, general arrays, dynamic values, or arbitrary native ABI layout facts.

capabilities.summary: No generated public C header, stable export symbol set, ownership boundary, or ABI compatibility promise exists.
capabilities.scope: E7 and E10 own generated headers, wrappers, manifests, independent consumers, and stabilization.
limitations: Private generated headers and target-facing c.* contracts are not a public ABI.

capabilities.summary: A version-locked public raylib.raw core is generated from Clang declaration facts and consumed directly by generated Haxe/C.
capabilities.scope: The coherent Caxecraft core covers 7 by-value records, the direct Camera/Camera3D struct alias, 5 integer enum domains with 143 constants, and 54 fixed-arity raylib 6.0 functions. A closed selection and binding lock retain upstream/header/Clang/target/declaration provenance; deterministic raw Haxe, five build-plan snapshots, a generated direct consumer, and a compiled independent ABI probe remain runtime-free.
limitations: This is a precise coherent subset, not complete raylib.h coverage or a general bindgen claim.
Callbacks, variadics, borrowed or retained pointers, resource ownership, and the remaining header families are explicitly omitted with owners.
Other Haxe targets, public package extraction, stable ABI, and the playable Caxecraft shell remain later work.

capabilities.summary: A curated raylib facade adds typed domains, direct value constructors, named colors, and inline forwarding with zero runtime cost.
capabilities.scope: The C-only semantic core wraps the locked raw slice with transparent value abstracts, five distinct integer domains, 26 named color macros, and direct PascalCase forwarding. Deterministic emitted-C snapshots prove imported struct compound literals and the same raylib calls with no allocation, reflection, virtual dispatch, or hxrt selection; negative fixtures reject mixed domains, embedded NUL, invalid color literals, invalid dimension types, and use of deliberately omitted resource-loading APIs.
limitations: The semantic facade is currently available only under the C custom target; target-neutral Caxecraft logic must stay above the rendering/input adapter.
Callbacks, variadics, pointers, native handles, resource ownership, and RAII-style scope helpers remain raw-only or omitted until their lifetime and cleanup contracts are proven.
Dynamic color-channel conversion is not guessed; Color.rgba currently requires compiler-visible values that fit c.UInt8.

capabilities.summary: The Reflaxe adapter captures complete typed modules and routes admitted output through request-local compiler state.
capabilities.scope: Typed input is captured before callback filtering and compiler-server requests clear all pending/current storage.
limitations: Adapter activation does not mean every typed declaration or expression is lowerable.

capabilities.summary: No publishable compiler package, signed reproducible archive, supported version, or release automation exists.
capabilities.scope: Current package metadata and package-layout probes are development inputs only.
limitations: Generated-output and runtime redistribution treatment also remains a separate pre-release decision.

capabilities.summary: The bounded native array slice provides checked contiguous growth and typed primitive/reference lifecycle operations.
capabilities.scope: Strict C11 evidence covers unboxed primitive and reference elements, relocation-safe aliasing, overflow, allocator failure, lifecycle rollback, owner move, and reverse destruction.
limitations: The array feature remains native-seed-only; generated Haxe Array lowering and standard-library collection parity remain unsupported.
Fixed arrays and spans remain runtime-free, while object descriptors, tracing collection, and stable public layouts are separately owned.

capabilities.summary: A typed deterministic feature graph resolves exact runtime closure under a versioned, provenance-locked internal ABI contract.
capabilities.scope: The schema-3 catalog pins per-feature semantic rationale, roots, documentation/evidence, internal ABI 0.5.0, same-major generated-C checks, forbidden application-export runtime types, source hashes, and strict C/C++ build baselines; schema-2 plans prove runtime-free programs or select exactly the justified closure.
limitations: Only the bounded literal-output edge currently selects hxrt; unsupported neighboring Haxe semantics still fail during lowering rather than being guessed as runtime blockers.
Runtime ABI 0.5.0 is an internal same-major contract; it does not expose or stabilize a generated application public ABI and does not establish a supported release.

capabilities.summary: Hardened native allocator, UTF-8 scalar string, typed array, status, and hosted output slices coexist with other provisional runtime seeds.
capabilities.scope: Allocator ownership, string semantics, typed resizable arrays, hosted length-delimited output, C/C++ header consumption, drift-checked source contracts, and exact selected packages validate only bounded split runtime slices.
limitations: Only literal hosted output selects a dependency-closed subset; there is still no unconditional runtime core or general runtime fallback.
The allocator, string, and array structs are versioned internal native-seed contracts and are explicitly forbidden from a generated application public C ABI.

capabilities.summary: The bounded native string slice enforces valid UTF-8, scalar indexing, allocation-aware ownership, and explicit CString lifetimes.
capabilities.scope: Strict C11 native evidence covers literal, checked/lossy decode, concat, slice, compare, hash, builder, and borrowed/owned CString primitives.
limitations: Generated literal output selects only the independent string-literal carrier; the full string feature remains native-seed-only and general String lowering remains unsupported.
The private runtime structs and symbols are not a stable exported C ABI, and broader Unicode algorithms remain separately owned.

capabilities.summary: General Haxe standard-library parity is not implemented.
capabilities.scope: Only exact compiler-recognized slices are covered: Std.int plus compiler-known literal Sys.println and default trace in hosted builds.
limitations: General String APIs, collections, Bytes/I/O, Std/Math/Type/Reflect, JSON, regex, time, sys, networking, and threading remain roadmap work.

capabilities.summary: The exact pinned Haxe public standard-library surface has a deterministic ownership and parity ledger.
capabilities.scope: Typed probes inventory 183 applicable source modules and 2,311 public APIs, then assign implementation, profile, environment, candidate runtime, diagnostics, Beads, and test ownership with an exact generated CSV review view.
limitations: This implements inventory and drift governance, not the APIs: only the exact Std.int row is currently conformant and broad standard-library behavior remains unsupported.

capabilities.summary: Per-compilation C namespace ownership and deterministic exact/generated symbol finalization are implemented.
capabilities.scope: The registry validates authored names, escapes reserved generated forms, handles overload/specialization identity, and records collisions.
limitations: Stable export ABI names remain unratified and broader typed-AST symbol collection is incomplete.

capabilities.summary: A bounded Haxe enum graph emits private native enums or tagged unions and runs as runtime-free strict C11.
capabilities.scope: Fieldless enums, primitive payloads, closed primitive generic instances, checked exhaustive matching, and function-local finite recursive values lower through schema-10 HxcIR; generic instances enter the shared specialization key, reason, limit, and report contract.
limitations: Reference/aggregate payloads, broader generic class/reference arguments, identity/equality, escaping recursive values, reflection, and public ABI enums remain fail-closed.

capabilities.summary: The pinned Haxe 5 CustomTarget(c) carrier configures scalar Unicode facts and exactly-once target registration.
capabilities.scope: Cold, package, compiler-server, non-C isolation, conflict, legacy Cross, and fail-closed production paths are tested.
limitations: Successful activation establishes target typing facts, not general lowering or platform support.

capabilities.summary: Typed c.* pointer, span, ownership, integer, Float32, layout, linkage, and metadata contracts type-check.
capabilities.scope: The contract macro validates the current declaration/build surface; explicit Float/c.Float32 conversion, literal and bounded compile-time-sized zero-initialized CArray/local Span operations, nonescaping Span or ConstSpan parameters on reached direct static implementation calls, and exact reached direct imports have separate admitted lowering slices.
limitations: Only the separately listed bounded CArray/Span and direct-import subsets lower; span parameters are not a public ABI, dynamic/heap fixed-array storage and most c.* operations have no runtime body or compiler lowering, and Syntax/Unsafe intentionally expose no authority.

capabilities.summary: No Haxe threading, synchronization, TLS, or atomic capability is advertised or implemented.
capabilities.scope: Target setup deliberately leaves target.threaded and target.atomics absent until an adapter proves them.
limitations: The presence of C11 atomic AST nodes or c.Atomic contracts is not Haxe concurrency support.

capabilities.summary: Haxe 5.0.0-preview.1, Reflaxe, Lix, package metadata, checksums, and provenance are pinned and verified.
capabilities.scope: Fresh package resolution and installed/source-checkout lifecycle probes use the reviewed dependency contract.
limitations: Pinned development dependencies are not a packaged or supported compiler release.

capabilities.summary: Complete typed-module capture and deterministic normalized ownership survive order changes and compiler-server reuse.
capabilities.scope: The adapter retains raw compiler objects while serializing only path-stable module, declaration, metadata, field, expression-root, and entry facts.
limitations: Typed-input capture is not semantic lowering, and unsupported nodes still fail with HXC1001.

capabilities.summary: Repository-owned Haxe has a source-exact guard against unreviewed untyped compiler seams.
capabilities.scope: The inventory scans Dynamic, Any, Reflect, untyped, open-ended carriers, casts, private access, raw injection, and JSON ingress while requiring closed typed models and negative evidence for each exception.
limitations: This protects compiler implementation technique; it does not implement user-language Dynamic or reflection semantics.

capabilities.summary: Namespaced typed-C declaration and build metadata produce a deterministic validated structural snapshot.
capabilities.scope: The macro rejects unknown metadata, invalid literals, duplicate names, unsafe paths, opaque-by-value use, cycles, and unsupported static assertions.
limitations: A valid contract snapshot does not itself emit a public header, select runtime code, or prove native layout.

capabilities.summary: Reachable ordinary class methods support deterministic minimal closed-world virtual dispatch while statically known calls remain direct.
capabilities.scope: Non-generic infallible class methods lower through schema-10 HxcIR with explicit self, root-only reachable slots, constructed-class tables, typed receiver adapters, exact override representation validation, and a conditional explanatory dispatch report; the generated strict C11 remains runtime-free and has no public exports.
limitations: Interfaces, closures, dynamic or generic methods/classes, escaping allocation, exception-bearing virtual methods, reflection/type tests, public class layouts, and stable method ABI remain fail-closed.
The generated table structs, object header pointer, adapters, identifiers, and hxc.dispatch.json report are private compiler artifacts rather than public ABI.
